name: Vietnam Off-Road: Ta Xua, Ha Giang and Cao Bang by Motorbike 10 days
duration: Places:Mu Cang Chai - Sapa - Ha Giang - Dong Van -  Cao Bang - Lang Son
highlights: Ride along legendary mountain passes like O Quy Ho and Ma Pi Leng, where jaw-dropping landscapes unfold at every turn.
Discover the traditions of Hmong, Tay, Nung, and Flower Hmong communities through village visits and colorful local markets.
Conquer challenging off-road tracks through remote valleys, tea hills, and terraced rice fields for a true adrenaline rush.
Marvel at natural wonders including the cloud-draped Dinosaur Backbone in Ta Xua and the thundering Ban Gioc Waterfall on the Vietnam - China border.

itinerary.title: Hanoi Motorcycle Tours to Ta Xua (Son La)
itinerary.description:
Distance & Est. time: 210 km

Morning

We kick off our adventure by leaving Hanoi and cruising along a weaving dyke-road that hugs the Red River toward Trung Ha Bridge. This route offers a refreshing escape from the city bustle, with shimmering waters on one side and glimpses of daily riverside life on the other. The air feels fresher with every kilometer as the urban chaos gives way to open skies and flowing countryside. From there, the ride takes us onto National Highway 32, where the landscape transforms into a painter’s masterpiece of endless paddy fields, tree-lined country roads, and jag...

itinerary.title: Ta Xua Motorbike Tour to Mu Cang Chai (Yen Bai)
itinerary.description:
Distance & Est. time: 190 km

After breakfast, we begin our ride at 08:00, rolling down the winding mountain road back toward Bac Yen town. The morning air is crisp, and mist still lingers over the valleys, giving the ride a dreamlike quality. From Bac Yen, we follow National Road 37 along the mighty Da River, its vast waters shimmering beneath towering cliffs. Passing through Muong Chum to It Ong, the ride is both peaceful and dramatic, with quiet fishing villages and endless mountains reflecting in the river’s glassy surface

itinerary.title: Mu Cang Chai Motorbike Tour to Sapa (Lao Cai)
itinerary.description:
Distance & Est. time: 140 km

Morning

At 09:00, after a hearty breakfast, we set off from Mu Cang Chai, first rolling along quiet backroads that wind between hills and valleys. Soon, the warm-up ride gives way to real challenges - rugged dirt tracks cutting through the mountains and remote Hmong villages, where life unfolds in harmony with a harsh yet breathtaking landscape. This ride offers intimate glimpses into daily life, from terraced fields carved by hand to villagers tending crops under the weight of unforgiving terrain and weather. By midday, we pause in Than Uyen, a tranquil town sur...

itinerary.title: Sapa Motorcycle Tour to Bac Ha (Lao Cai)
itinerary.description:
Distance & Est. time: 120 km

Leaving the misty heights of Sapa, we begin our descent by motorcycle towards Lao Cai City, a bustling border hub that connects Vietnam with China. Here, we pause for a refreshing coffee-stop on the bank of the Nam Thi River, where the tranquil flow of water contrasts with the lively border crossing nearby. As you sip your coffee, you can gaze across the river into Chinese territory, a rare moment of stillness that offers both scenic beauty and a sense of cultural connection. The ride continues as we follow Road 70 before turning onto Road 153, winding through val...

itinerary.title: Bac Ha Motorcycle Tours to Ha Giang City via Hoang Su Phi
itinerary.description:
Distance & Est. time: 200 km

Morning

After breakfast, we rev up our engines and leave Bac Ha behind, taking winding backroads that gradually reveal the raw beauty of Vietnam’s northern frontier. Our first highlight is Xin Man, a rugged district that sits right on the border with China. Hidden away among wild mountains and dense forests, Xin Man feels like a place frozen in time, untouched by mass tourism. The air here is crisp, the scenery unspoiled, and the sense of isolation only adds to its mystique

itinerary.title: Ha Giang Motorcycle Tours to Dong Van
itinerary.description:
Distance & Est. time: 160 km

After obtaining the special travel permit from Ha Giang Immigration Police, we set off on our motorbike adventure deeper into the borderlands. The road quickly turns into a thrilling zig-zag track, challenging yet rewarding, with every bend opening up new panoramas of limestone peaks, misty valleys, and vibrant hill-tribe hamlets. The landscape here is both harsh and poetic, shaped by time and resilience. Our ride takes us through Bac Sum Pass and the iconic Tham Ma Pass, two of the most breathtaking mountain passes in Ha Giang

itinerary.title: Dong Van Motorcycle Tours to Cao Bang
itinerary.description:
Distance & Est. time: 210 km

Morning

After breakfast, we set off from Dong Van and immediately face the legendary Ma Pi Leng Pass, one of the most dramatic mountain passes in all of Vietnam. Towering cliffs plunge steeply to the emerald ribbon of the Nho Que River below, while clouds drift lazily across jagged limestone peaks. Words barely capture its magnificence - this breathtaking scene is like a living painting, both awe-inspiring and humbling to behold. For those who wish, a serene boat ride on the Nho Que River offers the chance to glide through deep gorges and marvel at the vertical c...

itinerary.title: Cao Bang Off-Road Motorbike Loop Tour to Ban Gioc Waterfall
itinerary.description:
Distance & Est. time: 200 km

This morning, we begin a thrilling off-road ride through the highlands of Cao Bang Province, winding along mountain passes and valleys that reveal sweeping panoramas at every turn. The route is gentle but full of blind curves, which makes the ride both exciting and scenic. We can stop very often in places like Nguyen Binh and Tinh Tuc, where terraced fields, misty ridges, and traditional villages present endless opportunities for photography and quiet moments of admiration. Leaving town behind, the road carries us upward toward the graceful curves of Ma Phuc Pass

itinerary.title: Cao Bang Motorcycle Tour to Lang Son City
itinerary.description:
Distance & Est. time: 120 km

Morning

Today’s ride follows the legendary Highway 4, a road etched deeply into Vietnam’s history. As we cruise along this iconic route, we pass through Dong Khe Town, forever remembered for the fierce Battle of Dong Khe during the First Indochina War in September 1950. The terrain here still whispers stories of strategy and sacrifice, where the Viet Minh secured a decisive victory that shifted the course of history. By noon, we arrive in Lang Son City, a bustling border hub that hums with the energy of trade and cultural exchange

itinerary.title: Lang Son Motorcycle Tour Back to Hanoi
itinerary.description:
Distance & Est. time: 180 km

This final stretch of the journey begins in Lang Son City. If you wish, you can stroll once more through Dong Kinh Market, where the bustling stalls brim with inexpensive goods, local specialties, and Chinese imports - a perfect chance to pick up a few souvenirs. From Lang Son, we follow the smooth Highway 1B, an easier route that allows you to relax and savor the changing scenery as limestone karsts gradually give way to the fertile Red River Delta. We roll back into Hanoi by mid-afternoon, arriving before the rush hour around 15:00

included: Motorbike(s) (Honda or Yamaha). The surcharge to upgrade to Honda XR 150cc & Honda CRF 250cc is US$22/bike/day, US$34/bike/day and US$ 45/bike/day respectively
Basic riding gears of Helmet(s), elbow + knee pads and gloves
Accommodation as indicated in the itinerary
Meals as indicated in the itinerary
importantNotes: PROGRAMS & SCHEDULES SUBJECT TO CHANGE WITHOUT PRIOR NOTICE
